basics.name: Ivan Rybokolnikov
basics.label: Senior Backend & Systems Engineer
basics.summary: Senior Backend / Systems Engineer focused on high-load distributed systems, OLTP transactions, and modular monoliths. I specialize in authoritative backends (Node.js/TypeScript) where the server is the single source of truth, state mutates constantly, and data consistency is critical. Actively utilizing Golang to offload CPU-bound bottlenecks and eliminate event loop lag. Looking for complex highload challenges in a rigorous engineering culture.

work.summary: Played a key architectural role in scaling a platform from MVP to production. Architected the off-chain authoritative backend and completely rebuilt the client-side UI layer to deliver fault-tolerant business mechanics.
highlights: Engineered real-time domain systems and highly concurrent, transactional virtual economy subsystems.
Optimized DB I/O by condensing session lifecycles into 8 atomic transactions, ensuring strict ACID compliance.
Built a dynamic runtime geofencing engine with a custom state manager (deferred saves and batching) to prevent DB saturation.
Architected a Golang microservice via NATS to offload CPU-bound spatial calculations from the Node.js event loop, eliminating lag during peak loads.
Designed multi-stage state machines with strict server-side transitions and a session sandboxing system (distributed RBAC, dynamic TTLs).
Established a DI-based modular UI architecture, migrating legacy pipeline to Vite + pnpm and slashing CSS bundle from 20MB to 2MB.
Engineered a custom event-wrapper for reliable bi-directional routing between web interfaces (CEF), native 3D bindings (V8), and the backend.
